meta.title: Report Writing Skeletons
meta.description: Complete skeleton structures for Cambridge B2/C1 report writing
meta.format: 4-paragraph structure: Introduction → Current Situation → Problems → Recommendations

skeleton.title: Introduction
lines: State the aim and scope of the report
Explain how information was gathered (survey, questionnaire)
Mention the number of respondents and who they are

skeleton.title: Current Situation
lines: Describe what facilities are currently available
Highlight what is working well with evidence
Give a balanced, objective general assessment

skeleton.title: Problems
lines: Identify the main concern expressed by students
Give specific details and causes of the problem
Explain the impact on students' experience

skeleton.title: Recommendations
lines: Propose a clear, actionable primary recommendation
Add a supporting or secondary recommendation
State the expected benefit if changes are made

skeleton.title: Introduction
lines: State the aim of the report and name the programme
Mention location, duration, and number of participants
Explain the basis of the evaluation

skeleton.title: Accommodation & Activities
lines: Describe the quality of host families or accommodation
Outline the cultural and educational activities
Highlight the most successful elements

skeleton.title: Learning Outcomes
lines: Explain how language or academic skills improved
Describe personal development and confidence gained
Comment on cultural understanding achieved

skeleton.title: Recommendations
lines: State clearly whether the programme should continue
Suggest specific improvements for future participants
End with the overall value of the experience

skeleton.title: Introduction
lines: State the purpose: assess facilities for young people
Mention the survey basis and number of respondents
Define the target age group and focus

skeleton.title: Existing Facilities
lines: Describe current courts, pools, and gym equipment
Comment on what is in reasonable or good condition
Give balanced assessment of overall provision

skeleton.title: Improvements Needed
lines: Identify the main complaint (e.g. outdated equipment)
Mention opening hours, pricing, or access issues
Explain the impact on youth participation

skeleton.title: Recommendations
lines: Propose modernization of key equipment or areas
Suggest pricing or scheduling changes for students
State the expected increase in participation

skeleton.title: Introduction
lines: State the aim: evaluate a school trip
Give destination, duration, and number of students
Mention when it took place

skeleton.title: Accommodation & Activities
lines: Describe the accommodation quality
Outline the main educational activities
Highlight the most successful or enjoyable elements

skeleton.title: Feedback & Problems
lines: Start with what students enjoyed most
Identify specific issues (travel time, schedule, meals)
Explain how these problems affected the experience

skeleton.title: Recommendations
lines: Propose practical changes for future trips
Suggest adjustments to schedule or accommodation
State whether similar trips should continue

skeleton.title: Introduction
lines: State the purpose: review library services
Mention survey size and user group
Define what aspects are being assessed

skeleton.title: Current Services
lines: Describe available books, computers, and opening hours
Highlight positive features (helpful staff, quiet areas)
Give a balanced general assessment

skeleton.title: Problems Identified
lines: Name the main issue (e.g. closes too early)
Add supporting concerns (computers, seating, heating)
Explain the impact on students' study habits

skeleton.title: Recommendations
lines: Propose extended hours or additional resources
Suggest digital access or seating improvements
Emphasize why these improvements are essential

skeleton.title: Introduction
lines: State the aim: evaluate a work experience placement
Name the company, role, and duration
Specify when the placement took place

skeleton.title: Company & Tasks
lines: Describe the company size and industry
Outline the main tasks and responsibilities
Comment on the working environment

skeleton.title: Skills Developed
lines: Explain which professional skills improved
Describe personal development (communication, time management)
Highlight the most valuable learning experience

skeleton.title: Recommendations
lines: State whether the placement should continue
Suggest who would benefit most from this placement
Propose any improvements for future participants

skeleton.title: Introduction
lines: State the purpose: assess cafeteria services
Mention feedback basis and number of respondents
Define what is being assessed (food, service, pricing)

skeleton.title: Current Situation
lines: Describe current menu options and pricing
Comment on seating, queue times, and popularity
Give a balanced general assessment

skeleton.title: Main Complaints
lines: Identify the primary concern (e.g. lack of variety)
Add further complaints (vegetarian options, cold food)
Explain how these issues affect students

skeleton.title: Recommendations
lines: Propose expanded menu or vegetarian options
Suggest operational changes to reduce queues
State the expected improvement in student satisfaction

skeleton.title: Introduction
lines: State the aim: evaluate an environmental project
Name the initiative and when it ran
Mention the number of active participants

skeleton.title: Project Activities
lines: Describe the main activities carried out
Mention specific targets or installations
Comment on the reach and scale of the project

skeleton.title: Achievements & Difficulties
lines: Highlight measurable achievements (waste reduction, awards)
Identify challenges faced (resistance, budget, time)
Give a balanced assessment of the outcomes

skeleton.title: Recommendations
lines: Recommend continuing or expanding the project
Suggest embedding it into the curriculum
State the long-term benefits clearly

skeleton.title: Introduction
lines: State the purpose: review youth club facilities
Mention survey basis and current/former members
Define the target age group and location

skeleton.title: Current Facilities & Activities
lines: Describe available games, art, sport, and social areas
Comment on the condition of equipment
Give an overall assessment of current provision

skeleton.title: Issues Identified
lines: Identify the primary problem (limited activities)
Add further issues (Wi-Fi, opening hours, equipment)
Explain the impact on membership and attendance

skeleton.title: Recommendations
lines: Propose specific equipment or programme upgrades
Suggest extended hours or new activity types
State the expected growth in membership

skeleton.title: Introduction
lines: State the aim: evaluate a language course
Mention the language, location, and duration
Give class size and course level

skeleton.title: Teaching & Materials
lines: Describe teacher qualifications and approach
Comment on resources and learning materials used
Highlight the most effective teaching methods

skeleton.title: Effectiveness & Problems
lines: Explain how speaking or language skills improved
Identify problems (pace, grammar explanations, intensity)
Give a balanced assessment of the course

skeleton.title: Recommendations
lines: Propose improvements to level assessment or pacing
Suggest additional support sessions or break times
State whether the course is worth attending
